testcase.name: StynxAuthController > creates a session from the tenant header and token body
StynxAuthController > defaults session device metadata when the body omits it
StynxAuthController > rejects session creation without a tenant header
StynxAuthController > rejects session creation without a cognito token
StynxAuthController > switches tenant using the request claims and optional cognito subject
StynxAuthController > prefers the body tenant id when switching sessions
StynxAuthController > rejects session switching without request claims or tenant
StynxAuthController > logs out the current session and returns ok
StynxAuthController > rejects logout without a session claim
StynxAuthController > delegates inspect and invalidate actions
StynxAuthModule > builds the dynamic module with the expected controller, providers, and exports
StynxAuthModule > returns a null cache backend when redis support is disabled
StynxAuthModule > resolves auth options with defaults and optional redis/permission overrides
auth runtime helpers > patches actor context only when stynx claims are present
auth runtime helpers > allows public and system routes and enforces permission membership otherwise
auth runtime helpers > short-circuits permission checks for route metadata defaults
auth runtime helpers > hydrates request auth context and readonly state from a valid bearer token
auth runtime helpers > rejects missing bearer tokens and inactive sessions
auth runtime helpers > manages in-memory permission cache records and metrics snapshots
auth runtime helpers > handles jwt and permission utility helpers deterministically
StynxAuthService > exchanges a cognito token using an existing user and active request context
StynxAuthService > creates a user when cognito subject is unseen and there is no active request context
StynxAuthService > creates a request context when only the mutator provider is available
StynxAuthService > reuses the current email when an existing user is found and cognito omits email
StynxAuthService > switches tenant, revokes the old session, and invalidates the old sid
StynxAuthService > uses the stynx subject when switching without a cognito subject
StynxAuthService > logs out, inspects, and invalidates permissions through the cache
StynxAuthService > fails clearly when the database or session service provider is unavailable
StynxAuthService > fails clearly when the database provider is unavailable during cognito exchange
@stynx/auth barrel > exports at least one symbol
mapCognitoError > maps UserNotFoundException → IDENTITY_NOT_FOUND
mapCognitoError > maps ResourceNotFoundException → IDENTITY_NOT_FOUND
mapCognitoError > maps NotAuthorizedException → IDENTITY_FORBIDDEN
mapCognitoError > maps AccessDeniedException → IDENTITY_FORBIDDEN
mapCognitoError > maps UsernameExistsException → IDENTITY_CONFLICT
mapCognitoError > maps AliasExistsException → IDENTITY_CONFLICT
mapCognitoError > maps TooManyRequestsException → IDENTITY_RATE_LIMITED
mapCognitoError > maps InvalidParameterException → IDENTITY_VALIDATION_ERROR
mapCognitoError > maps InvalidPasswordException → IDENTITY_VALIDATION_ERROR
mapCognitoError > maps SomeOtherException → IDENTITY_PROVIDER_ERROR
mapCognitoError > produces a default IDENTITY_PROVIDER_ERROR for unknown shapes
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > constructs with an explicit endpoint override
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listUsers calls ListUsersCommand with the email filter when present
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listUsers uses the phone filter when only phone is set
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listUsers routes through ListUsersInGroupCommand when group is set
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listUsers maps empty grouped responses with pagination token
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listUsers maps empty ungrouped responses without pagination
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listUsers omits optional summary fields when Cognito omits them
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listUsers falls back to username attributes when Cognito omits Username
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listUsers clamps limit to 60 when caller requests more
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listUsers maps mapCognitoError on failure
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > getUser calls AdminGetUserCommand and returns a IdentityUserDetail
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > getUser preserves blank attribute values and optional update metadata
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > getUser handles an undefined attributes array
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > getUserBySubject lists users by sub filter then fetches details
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > getUserBySubject throws IDENTITY_NOT_FOUND when nothing matches
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > getUserBySubject preserves IdentityAdminError thrown from getUser
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > getUserBySubject maps provider failures while listing by subject
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > updateUser composes the right attribute list including custom: prefix
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > updateUser short-circuits attribute push when nothing changes
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > disableUser / enableUser issue the right commands
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listGroupsForUser maps response.Groups and filters blanks
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listGroupsForUser handles missing groups and descriptions
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listGroups paginates via NextToken and maps items
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listGroups handles missing groups and omits absent pagination
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listGroups preserves group descriptions when present
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > addUserToGroup / removeUserFromGroup issue correct commands
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > resetUserPassword + setUserPassword issue correct commands
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > verifyUserChannels marks email_verified + phone_number_verified
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > verifyUserChannels skips the call when neither channel is requested
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > resolveCredentials default-chain returns undefined (uses AWS SDK default)
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > resolveCredentials with strategy=profile throws when no profile is set
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > resolveCredentials with strategy=provided throws without explicit credentials
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > resolveCredentials with strategy=provided + credentials accepts them
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > username fallback uses the configured order when Username is undefined
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > username fallback returns an empty string when no fallback attributes are present
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > resolveCredentials with strategy=profile accepts explicit profile names
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > getUser maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > updateUser maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > disableUser maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > enableUser maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listGroupsForUser maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > listGroups maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > addUserToGroup maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > removeUserFromGroup maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > resetUserPassword maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > setUserPassword maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
CognitoIdentityAdminAdapter > verifyUserChannels maps provider failures to IdentityAdminError
buildCognitoAdminOptionsFromEnv > reads region + userPoolId from environment
buildCognitoAdminOptionsFromEnv > falls back to AWS_REGION and AWS_DEFAULT_REGION
buildCognitoAdminOptionsFromEnv > honors overrides over env
buildCognitoAdminOptionsFromEnv > throws IDENTITY_VALIDATION_ERROR when region is missing
buildCognitoAdminOptionsFromEnv > throws IDENTITY_VALIDATION_ERROR when userPoolId is missing
buildCognitoAdminOptionsFromEnv > uses profile strategy when AWS_PROFILE is set
buildCognitoAdminOptionsFromEnv > respects STYNX_IDENTITY_ADMIN_CREDENTIALS_STRATEGY env var
buildCognitoAdminOptionsFromEnv > includes endpoint, credentials, and fallback-order overrides when provided
CognitoTokenVerifier — constructor > defaults jwksUri to <issuer>/.well-known/jwks.json
CognitoTokenVerifier — constructor > honors explicit jwksUri override
CognitoTokenVerifier — constructor > uses default claim names when none provided
CognitoTokenVerifier — constructor > honors custom claim names
CognitoTokenVerifier — header-shape early validation > throws on undefined header
CognitoTokenVerifier — header-shape early validation > throws on header without Bearer prefix
CognitoTokenVerifier — header-shape early validation > throws on empty array
CognitoTokenVerifier — header-shape early validation > throws on array whose first element lacks Bearer prefix
CognitoTokenVerifier — header-shape early validation > verifies a signed bearer token and maps principal metadata
CognitoTokenVerifier — header-shape early validation > omits optional principal and timestamp metadata when claims are absent
CognitoTokenVerifier — payload branch helpers > resolves principals, optional profile claims, and de-duplicated authorization claims
CognitoTokenVerifier — payload branch helpers > accepts audience matches through aud, client_id, or azp and rejects mismatches
CognitoTokenVerifier — payload branch helpers > enforces token_use only when configured
CognitoTokenVerifier — payload branch helpers > creates and reuses the remote JWKS resolver
auth decorators > attaches public, system, readonly, and permission metadata
verifyAuthMutationCoverage > tracks the six ADR-002 mutation paths exactly
EffectiveHashComputer > recomputes membership hashes for direct and grouped role mutations
EffectiveHashComputer > recomputes the active membership hash for a user and tenant pair
EffectiveHashComputer > short-circuits empty mutation inputs
EffectiveHashComputer > throws when the database provider is unavailable
InMemoryPermissionCacheBackend edge branches > ignores delete and malformed invalidation misses
InMemoryPermissionCacheBackend edge branches > publishes to subscribers and clears records on close
InMemoryPermissionCacheBackend edge branches > ignores tenant and user invalidation misses
PermissionCache > records metrics defensively when a tier lookup misses
PermissionCache > serves a matching in-memory record without recomputing and records the in-memory metric
PermissionCache > hydrates from the backend when the in-memory entry is absent and records the redis metric
PermissionCache > invalidates fan-out for 100 sessions in the same tenant
PermissionCache > falls back to DB resolution when the backend is unavailable and records the db tier metric
PermissionCache > rethrows backend failures when DB fallback is disabled
PermissionCache > recomputes when cached permissions do not match the token hash
PermissionCache > invalidates a single sid in memory and backend storage
PermissionCache > clears all cached sessions on global invalidation and ignores malformed invalidation messages
PermissionCache > invalidates only matching sessions for user and tenant scoped messages
PermissionCache > expires in-memory entries and clamps backend ttl to at least one second
PermissionCache > evicts the oldest in-memory entry when the local cache reaches capacity
PermissionCache > writes recomputed records to the backend with the one-day fallback ttl
PermissionCache > keeps a cached record when the drift probe has no active membership hash
PermissionCache > recomputes when the cached hash probe detects drift before querying the database again
PermissionCache > keeps wildcard invalidations scoped to the requested user or tenant
PermissionCache > closes the backend on module destroy
PermissionCache > proactively re-syncs stale in-memory permission entries
PermissionCache > swallows background re-sync failures for stale in-memory entries
PermissionCache > treats a cleared drift interval as zero during scheduled re-sync
PermissionQueryService > resolves membership permissions inside an existing request context
PermissionQueryService > creates a request context when none is active and supports probeHash
PermissionQueryService > creates a request context when the mutator exists but RequestContext is unavailable
PermissionQueryService > throws when membership is missing or database provider is unavailable
PermissionQueryService > runs without a request context mutator and issues the expected membership queries
PermissionQueryService > returns null hash and zero generation when probeHash finds no active membership
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > onModuleInit is a no-op when redis options are absent
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > onModuleInit does not reconnect when both redis clients are already open
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > onModuleInit creates, connects, subscribes, and forwards invalidation messages
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > subscribes immediately when a subscriber client is already attached
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > get returns null when no client is configured
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > get parses JSON value when present
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > get returns null when redis returns nothing
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > set is a no-op when no client is configured
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > set writes record + user/tenant indexes with TTL
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > delete is a no-op when no client is configured
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > delete removes record and (when known) sids from user/tenant indexes
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > delete only deletes the record when no record exists in the index
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > invalidateScope is a no-op when no client is configured
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > invalidateScope ignores malformed messages without both parts
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > invalidateScope handles per-user scope by listing user sids
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > invalidateScope leaves user-indexed records alone when tenant does not match
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > invalidateScope handles tenant-wide scope (userId=*)
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > invalidateScope handles global scope (*:*) by scanning keys
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > invalidateScope accepts scanIterator string keys as well as key arrays
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > subscribe records the handler and re-subscribes when subscriber is present
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > subscribe only records the handler when redis subscriber or options are absent
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > publish forwards messages on the configured invalidate channel
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > publish is a no-op without redis options or without a client
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > close quits both client and subscriber when open
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > close skips quit calls when clients are already closed
RedisPermissionCacheBackend > onModuleDestroy delegates to close
StynxAuthGuard > allows public and system routes without reading the request
StynxAuthGuard > rejects missing bearer tokens and inactive sessions
StynxAuthGuard > validates bearer tokens, resolves permissions, and writes request principal state
StynxAuthGuard > accepts authorization header arrays and response aliases without a session provider
auth utils > normalizes permissions before hashing
auth utils > expands wildcard grants deterministically and preserves explicit grants
auth utils > converts supported header forms to strings
auth utils > round-trips base64url values without padding and rejects malformed JWT shapes
auth utils > decodes JWT claims and expands wildcard grants with regex metacharacters literally
auth utils > verifies a valid RS256 JWT with the supplied JWK
auth utils > deduplicates trimmed wildcard universes and keeps unmatched grants explicit
auth validators > loads jose through the default lazy loader
auth validators > validates cognito access tokens and authorization headers
auth validators > accepts bearer header arrays and preserves optional cognito username/email claims
auth validators > trims cognito bearer tokens before validation
auth validators > returns empty cognito sub and omits username when optional identity claims are absent
auth validators > rejects invalid cognito token_use values and missing configuration
auth validators > validates stynx jwt claims using the injected signing service and remote jwks fallback
auth validators > falls back to remote jwks and rejects inactive or expired tokens
auth validators > forces a signing-service key refresh after an initial verification failure
auth validators > tries later jwks keys before refreshing and preserves empty stynx claim defaults
auth validators > reuses cached jwks until refresh is forced and rejects failed remote jwks loads
auth validators > rejects issuer mismatches and missing jwks configuration for stynx tokens
auth validators > rejects empty key sets and supports tokens without optional stynx claims
auth validators > rejects invalid signatures from verifyJwtWithJwk
auth validators > enforces stynx token time boundaries and refreshes expired remote jwks cache
auth validators > keeps signing-service jwks cached for the full configured lifetime
